title: Fuyutsuki Gendos Imaginary Friend Purpose and Morality
display_title: Fuyutsuki Gendos Imaginary Friend Purpose and Morality
wikitext:
[]: Fuyutsuki: Gendo's Imaginary Friend? [Purpose and Morality]             Quote Post by SharpenedMoonlight » Mon Mar 25, 2013 10:11 pm I took the liberty of changing the thread topic to emphasise the more serious side of the discussion - OMF I had an interesting conversation today with a friend about Fuyutsuki and exactly what his purpose is in the scheme of things. He's Gendo's second in command, but really he doesn't do much except be a silent stand-in. And even when he does things, it's normally just small things that tie up the loose ends made by Gendo. He only really talks to Gendo mostly, and it's usually metaphysical random babble. And when he does interact with others, it's usually quick, impersonal, and not very detailed, with the exception of in EOE where he takes command, but really he's just being Gendo's extra arms and legs there. Unlike Gendo he has no real power, no main duties, and he isn't a member of Seele (so it's odd that he even gets to watch their meetings.) Sure, he has a backstory, but the only real time that shows he exists as a seperate person is when he gets kidnapped by Seele, and even that is minor. Basically what I'm getting at is this: What if Fuyutsuki DOESN'T exist and is just a fake existence in Gendo's mind? A hallucination made to give Gendo some companionship and loyalty? Even the known backstory of Fuyutsuki only has to do with Gendo and Yui. Yui's dead, so there's nobody to change Gendo's story. Fuyutsuki's existence is based on Yui, Gendo, and Nerv, the same things as Gendo. Maybe Fuyutsuki is a seperate part of Gendo's psyche, one who watches himself deteriorate into evil and disapproves, but cannot stop it. What do you think? (I'm saying all this knowing Fuyutsuki is in fact a real person in the Neon Genesis Evangelion universe, I just thought it made interesting debate.)

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Mar 25, 2013 10:19 pm Fuyutsuki's contributions are vital to the Ikaris's agenda and humanity's survival. It might not look like the guy does much and it's certainly a shame such a stand-up and level headed man as him is not the commander, but to say most of what he does is minor or irrelevant is a bit much, in my opinion. It's true that he doesn't have an agenda of his own, but i fail to see why that is a flaw on his part. However, there is certainly a dynamic between Gendo and Fuyutsuki in the matter of morality and integrity. Fuyutsuki's willing to sacrifice his life for what he believes in and posseses very high moral standards, whereas Gendo is much more practical-minded and doesn't really care as much for people as Fuyutsuki does.

[]:             Quote Post by Lelouch34 » Mon Mar 25, 2013 11:52 pm Fuyutsuki can't be a part of Gendo's imagination when so my other characters interact with him. As for his purpose see the scene where Fuyutsuki says his loyalties lie with Yui, and her plan in Episode 21 directors cut. The best way in his mind is to folow Yui's plan is to join NERV to stop the Angels. Fuyutsuki also joins with the man who also wants to halt Seele's plan which is Gendo. He does this to stop Seele's plan which goes against Yui's while Gendo's does so as well. So it is also possible and probable he was there to stop Gendo if need be to prevent him from going against Yui's plan to prevent instrumentality. Fuyutsuki also wanted to stop Gendo from stealing Yui away again from him, and why he wanted to probably stop Gendo. Also, it would have beeen interesting to see Fuyusuki, and Shinji interact. That way we could understand more about his feelings towards Yui. Another question to think about how much did he care about Shinji? Did he want to help him or did he distance himself from Shinji because Shinji was first in Yui's heart? She the scene again as I stated above when Yui says that "everything I do is for Shinji's sake." While Shinji reaches for Yui and he holds him while Fuyutsuki watches with a jealous look on his face. Most likely he wanted to avoid Shinji for this reason. Still its clear that his main role deals a support at NERV, and some humor. While most importantly serving as a living connection to help us understand who Yui was a person through his memories. He was also most likely a man with some higher moral standards than Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 26, 2013 11:33 am I can already tell that this theory will probably go down in history as one of the all time greatest Eva spoof theories. However, there is a grain of truth here as Gendo and Fuyutsuki are a very frequent double act throughout the show. This was most famously parodied by the Statler and Waldorf, Evangelion Muppet Show Bumper . The two really are seen together an awful lot it must be said. But there are reasons for this.(I'm taking the opportunity to test the wkimg tag system again). [wkimg]01_C131.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]02_C267.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]05_C084b.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]06_C090.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]11_083a.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]12_C106.jpg[/wkimg][wkimg]13_C223a.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]14_C123.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]15_C005.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]17_C116a.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]18_C230e.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]19_C125Aa.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]19_C370a.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]20_C027.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]22_C230.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]22_C255.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg]24_C191.jpg[/wkimg] Fuyutsuki serves as the mouthpiece for the more taciturn Gendo, and as a means to draw Gendo into conversation on deeper "plot" issues concerning Seele, 3I, etc. He often also serves as an emotional mouthpiece for Gendo, articulating concerns or issues which Gendo himself would never offer alone. Fuyutsuki offers the audience deeper insight into Gendo, while still allowing Gendo to remain mostly silent and stern throughout. The resulting explosions of emotion from Gendo, usually concerning Rei, are all the more potent because of this. But Fuyutsuki has what is essentially his very own episode, #21', where his past and motivations are explored. He also has his own, very important monologues, which isn't typically what you'd expect from an "imaginary friend". Episode #14 (ADV) wrote: FUYUTSUKI:What about the Unit 00 accident? That wasn't in my script. IKARI:It is no obstacle. The re-synchronization test for Rei and Unit 00 was successful. FUYUTSUKI (MONO): Ikari is too concerned about Rei . [wkimg]14_C125b.jpg[/wkimg]

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Mar 26, 2013 12:23 pm Actually, he's more morally repugnant than Gendo. He knows what's going on around him is wrong, but willfully buys into Yui's insane scheme, and then buys into Gendo's ridiculous scenario so he can catch a few seconds glimpse of a woman that isn't even into him- and it's not even her . It's just Rei pretending to be her. Anno is critical of the younger generation and holds them up against the standard of a mythical nostalgic past, but Fuyutsuki proves that past doesn't exist; our problems aren't ours, they're our parents problems, thrust upon us without hope of resolving them until the next generation catches the shit. It all rolls down hill onto the pilots and Fuyutsuki is not only part of that, he's aware of and sardonic about how evil it all is and participates anyway. There may be some dissonance here in Japanese vs. Western values.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Tue Mar 26, 2013 12:29 pm Chuckman wrote: Actually, he's more morally repugnant than Gendo. He knows what's going on around him is wrong, but willfully buys into Yui's insane scheme, and then buys into Gendo's ridiculous scenario so he can catch a few seconds glimpse of a woman that isn't even into him- and it's not even her . It's just Rei pretending to be her. Anno is critical of the younger generation and holds them up against the standard of a mythical nostalgic past, but Fuyutsuki proves that past doesn't exist; our problems aren't ours, they're our parents problems, thrust upon us without hope of resolving them until the next generation catches the shit. It all rolls down hill onto the pilots and Fuyutsuki is not only part of that, he's aware of and sardonic about how evil it all is and participates anyway. There may be some dissonance here in Japanese vs. Western values.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Mar 26, 2013 1:10 pm He was able to converse with Seele and Kaji after his kidnapping. I think that constitutes as him being "real".

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Mar 26, 2013 4:59 pm Uh...in like episode 11, don't Ritsuko, Aoba, and Maya talk to Fuyutsuki on the train? Without Gendo anywhere in the vicinity?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 26, 2013 5:33 pm OK, I think since everyone is agreed that Fuyutsuki is in fact real, we may as well discuss Fuyutsuki's general role/purpose in Evangelion, as per SharpenedMoonlight's original conversation. I'm particularly interested in conversations about Fuyutsuki's moral role in Eva, both in positive and Negative aspects. This has been mentioned a few times, but does anyone have additional thoughts on this?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 01, 2013 9:26 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'm particularly interested in conversations about Fuyutsuki's moral role in Eva, both in positive and Negative aspects.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Apr 02, 2013 12:29 pm Reichu wrote: A Third Impact was deemed inevitable, unpreventable. Faced with this dilemma, Yui Ikari determined that the only way to win against Seele -- who controlled the world, more or less -- was to stay on the inside, be indispensible, and become masters of Seele's own tools, waiting for the critical moment to gut the old men's plans from within.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2013 2:02 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Maybe Third Impact wasn't inevitable, only Seele's determination to go through with it was. Or the idea that a group like Seele might always emerge was itself inevitable?

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Tue Apr 02, 2013 7:55 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Uh...in like episode 11, don't Ritsuko, Aoba, and Maya talk to Fuyutsuki on the train? Without Gendo anywhere in the vicinity?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Apr 02, 2013 8:02 pm Elect G-Max wrote: It was one of those episodes, yeah. They have a conversation about the Magi being the de facto government of the city of Tokyo-3 or whatever. So he's real.

[]: Re: Fuyutsuki: Gendo's Imaginary Friend? [Purpose and Morali             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Apr 10, 2013 12:09 am Just to clarify, OP always knew that Fuyutsuki was a real person. SharpenedMoonlight wrote: (I'm saying all this knowing Fuyutsuki is in fact a real person in the Neon Genesis Evangelion universe, I just thought it made interesting debate.)

[]: Re: Fuyutsuki: Gendo's Imaginary Friend? [Purpose and Morali             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Apr 10, 2013 5:59 pm Dream wrote: Just to clarify, OP always knew that Fuyutsuki was a real person. How basically everyone missed this is honestly beyond me.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Apr 12, 2013 1:12 pm Amusing reactions above. To be fair, the way the post is formatted it's not difficult to overlook the last statement, even if it is in brackets. Somehow I land up with the impression that Fuyu is a semi stand-in for Yui in serving as Gendo's conscience. Both of them do have Yui as their connection, and they're both determined to see her again - Gendo more than Fuyu, but anyway. A lot of what Fuyu says and does depends on what Yui was up to during her days - and Yui being inscrutable, it's hard to say what Yui was up to. I know it's beyond the topic of the conversation, but looking back at the series, I get the funny feeling that Fuyutsuki was set up in his present position by Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Fri Apr 12, 2013 3:47 pm Is it possible that Yui actually wanted SEELE's scenario to come to fruition?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 12, 2013 6:06 pm LeoXiao wrote: Is it possible that Yui actually wanted SEELE's scenario to come to fruition?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Apr 12, 2013 6:10 pm UrsusArctos wrote: I know it's beyond the topic of the conversation, but looking back at the series, I get the funny feeling that Fuyutsuki was set up in his present position by Yui.
